title: Non-monotonic evolution of multipartite entanglement under the Unruh effect
authors: Shu-Min Wu, Si-Han Shang, Si-Yu Liu, Rui-Yang Xu, Qianqian Liu, Xiao-Li Huang
abstract: We investigate the behavior of tetrapartite entanglement in a four-qubit Dicke state under relativistic motion by employing the Unruh-DeWitt detector model, where one detector undergoes uniform acceleration. We show that the entanglement exhibits a non-monotonic evolution: it first decreases and subsequently increases toward a finite value as the acceleration grows. In contrast to the conventional view that the Unruh effect leads to a monotonic degradation of multipartite entanglement, our results demonstrate that it can instead enhance multipartite entanglement within a finite parameter regime. This behavior reveals a dual role of the Unruh effect in multipartite systems. Our findings therefore provide a refined understanding of relativistic multipartite quantum correlations, indicating that Dicke states constitute more robust multipartite quantum resources against Unruh-induced decoherence and may offer advantages for relativistic quantum information processing tasks.
summary: This paper shows that the Unruh effect can non-monotonically evolve and even enhance multipartite entanglement in Dicke states. It demonstrates that Dicke states are more robust against Unruh-induced decoherence than GHZ or W states, making them superior resources for quantum information tasks in relativistic settings.
main_problem: The study investigates whether multipartite entanglement in Dicke states undergoes monotonic degradation under the Unruh effect and explores the robustness of these states compared to GHZ and W states.
main_result: The researchers found that tetrapartite entanglement exhibits a non-monotonic evolution, initially decreasing and then increasing toward a finite value, demonstrating that the Unruh effect can actually enhance multipartite entanglement in certain regimes.
method: The study employs the Unruh-DeWitt detector model within the weak-coupling regime, using first-order perturbation theory and Bogoliubov transformations to calculate the time-evolved density matrix.
model_or_system: A four-qubit Dicke state shared among four observers, where one detector (David) undergoes uniform acceleration in a massless scalar field in Minkowski spacetime while the others remain inertial.
key_observables: Tetrapartite entanglement (pi4-tangle), 1-3 bipartite entanglement (negativity), and 1-1 bipartite entanglement.
important_parameters: Acceleration parameter (q), detector energy gap (Omega), interaction time (Delta), and effective coupling strength (nu).
main_assumptions: The detectors are modeled as two-level systems, the interaction is localized via a Gaussian-profile smearing function, and the system is treated within the weak-coupling approximation.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the experimental setup; Figure 2 illustrates the divergence in bipartite entanglement behavior between inertial and accelerated observers; Figure 3 and 4 demonstrate the non-monotonic 'dip and recovery' of the pi4-tangle as a function of acceleration, energy gap, and interaction time.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of entanglement in non-inertial frames, describes the Unruh-DeWitt model and mathematical framework, presents results on bipartite and multipartite entanglement evolution, compares Dicke states to GHZ/W states, and discusses implications for relativistic quantum information.
why_interesting: It challenges the conventional view of entanglement degradation and provides a mechanism (mode mixing) for entanglement enhancement, which is highly relevant for understanding decoherence and resource management in relativistic quantum information and open quantum systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
